item.title: People with MS concerned about losing access to NDIS as survey reveals some already don't qualify for scheme
item.description: There are fears as the government seeks to achieve its budget savings with cutbacks to NDIS access, those who need support won't receive it. A new survey has found not all those with multiple sclerosis have access to the scheme - and for those who do, they face constant misunderstandings about their condition.

item.title: Inside the costly trend charging up Australia's classic car scene
item.description: A niche group of classic car owners are giving used vehicles an electric second life — but is it worth it?

item.title: Melbourne teenager becomes youngest Australian to summit Mount Everest
item.description: The 18-year-old's singular achievement comes as the number of climbers ascending to the world's tallest peak hits a record high.

item.title: He made a formula to mock his profession. Then it correctly predicted three World Cups
item.description: The football fan has correctly predicted the World Cup winner since 2014. Here's the side he's picked as this year's champions.

item.title: A 'safe haven' in times of turmoil. Why demand for this country's 'golden visa' is rising
item.description: New Zealand has long been popular among backpackers and thrill-seekers. Now wealthy investors are flocking there too.

item.title: $10bn wipeout: Why investors are suddenly worried about an Australian giant
item.description: CSL's dramatic collapse has shattered investor confidence in one of Australia's most trusted companies.

item.title: SBS sits down with independent MP Allegra Spender
item.description: SBS speaks with independent MP Allegra Spender to discuss the recent federal budget

item.title: INTERVIEW: May is Lupus Awareness Month. SBS has spoken to mental health advocate Jenny Lai about her journey
item.description: Every May, Lupus Awareness Month is recognised around the world, to highlight the chronic autoimmune disease which is still widely misunderstood. Lupus affects over five million people globally, and many of those affected are women. Jenny Lai is a mental health advocate whose Lupus diagnosis was a catalyst in her mental health journey. She has spoken to SBS' Alyssa Chandler.

item.title: How three Red Cross volunteers became the first victims of the latest Ebola outbreak
item.description: The Bundibugyo strain of Ebola was declared an emergency of international concern by the World Health Organization on Sunday.

item.title: Iranian football team moves FIFA World Cup base from United States to Mexico
item.description: Iran's participation in the World Cup has been in question since February, when the United States and Israel began bombing the country.

item.title: 'He thrives on controversy': The past and possible future of Israel's 'TikTok minister'
item.description: Itamar Ben-Gvir has risen to the highest level of Israeli politics, but he may not have a long future there.

item.title: They were lucky to survive — but one question still haunts them
item.description: For those left with guilt, trauma and grief, survival can be something some still struggle to make sense of.

item.title: 'Doesn't sound good': Fears for difficult citizenship choice under new Coalition policy
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has said that he would block non-citizens from access to 17 different welfare programs.

item.title: Australians returning home from Gaza-bound aid flotilla allege abuse by Israeli authorities
item.description: Israel has denied the allegations, saying the detainees were "held in accordance with the law".

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 25 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Flotilla activists allege abuse by Israeli authorities | Evening News Bulletin 25 May 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, fresh sexual assault claims from Gaza flotilla activists, ASIO defends its resourcing before the Bondi attack. And in football, Martin Boyle boosted by good club form, as he seeks his first Socceroos World Cup squad.

item.title: Teal independents in talks to set up their own party
item.description: Several so-called 'teal' independents are in advanced discussions about the potential formation of a new political party. Responding to growing distrust for the major parties and surging support for One Nation, some independent MPs say Australia needs a more sensible opposition.

item.title: 'A fighter': Tributes continue for AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner
item.description: A state memorial is being planned for Daniher, who has been remembered as "just incredibly selfless" and "a great family man".

item.title: Why oil prices will remain high even if the war ends, and why stamp prices to rise
item.description: Oil prices have tumbled after Donald Trump suggested a peace deal between the US and Iran could be near, raising hopes of lower fuel costs for Australian motorists. SBS On the Money speaks with MST Financial’s Saul Kavonic about why petrol prices may ease in the short term, but why oil markets are unlikely to return to pre-conflict levels anytime soon.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Stolen Generations survivor on significance of Sorry Day and ongoing fight for redress
item.description: The impacts of the Stolen Generation policies on families were detailed publicly for the first time in the landmark 'Bringing Them Home' report 29 years ago, following a national inquiry that featured the testimonies of more than 500 Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. Many recommendations from the report on redress and support for survivors still remain unimplemented. The Healing Foundation's David Wragge has spoken with Biwa Kwan about marking Sorry Day, and the work that remains to ensure appropriate redress and support is provided to ageing survivors. First Nations listeners seeking support can contact 13YARN on 13 92 76.

item.title: Israeli strike kills six-year-old girl and a woman in Gaza, medics say
item.description: Health authorities in the Gaza Strip say 900 people have died in attacks since a truce started in October.

item.title: The 'anomaly' that derailed Vivid Sydney's biggest-ever drone show
item.description: Here's where the "biggest drone show program in Vivid Sydney's history" went wrong

item.title: Energy bill relief: Here's how much power prices are set to fall
item.description: Residents and small businesses across four states could see their energy bills reduced in the new financial year.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 26 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: New Zealand property may be tempting Australians — but there's a major catch
item.description: While New Zealand's lack of a capital gains tax is turning heads after the federal budget, experts are urging caution.

item.title: Sorry won't cut it anymore: calls grow for action on Bringing Them Home recommendations
item.description: Almost three decades on from the landmark Bringing Them Home report on Stolen Generations, the government has implemented just six per cent of recommendations. This National Sorry Day, advocates are calling for government action to better support survivors and their families.

item.title: IS-linked families return to Australia from Syria: Here's what we know about them
item.description: Seven women with links to the self-proclaimed IS-group, and their children, have arrived weeks after three women were arrested.

item.title: Ebola numbers continue to rise
item.description: Cases of Ebola continue to rise in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of Congo, with the number of suspected cases approaching 1,000, and suspected deaths at more than 200. Aid groups are warning they need more funding to tackle the disease, and health workers and volunteers are working to educate people and quash misinformation.

item.title: What are the Abraham Accords - and why do they matter?
item.description: US President Donald Trump has called on a range of Gulf countries to improve their relationship with Israel as negotiations continue with Iran on an agreement to end the war. In a post on Truth Social, Trump has called on more Arab and Muslim states to sign up to the Abraham Accords, which were brokered during his first term in office. Here, we explain what the accords are, and why they're so significant.

item.title: Why New Zealand property is suddenly drawing attention in Australia
item.description: As Australians furiously debate changes to housing taxes, discussion has emerged about whether investors could look to New Zealand for more favourable tax treatments. That's because New Zealand does not impose a broad capital gains tax on investment properties like Australia does. But experts warn there's a catch.

item.title: Space economy hype grows, as businesses pull back on investments because of fuel costs
item.description: Australian businesses are feeling the pressure from rising fuel costs and supply issues, with new ABS data showing nearly three quarters have been negatively impacted. Business NSW Executive Director David Borger joins Ricardo Gonçalves on the SBS On the Money Podcast to discuss how companies are responding and what it means for prices and inflation ahead of key data tomorrow.

item.title: 'Still going in 2028': The issue that may stall US-Iran peace efforts for years
item.description: Trump has said that a peace deal with Iran is close but what exactly are the two countries in disagreement over?

item.title: 'Not what it used to be': Is the weather too hot for Euro summer travel?
item.description: Europe's heatwave is a sign of things to come. Will Australian tourists be deterred?

item.title: I was lost in the bush for 10 days with my son. A note left on a bench saved our lives
item.description: In 2017, Michelle Pittman and her son set out on a bushwalk, not realising that one wrong turn would lead to a fight for survival.

item.title: Autism: it's not just a male thing anymore
item.description: New research shows that the ratio between girls and boys with autism is much closer than once thought. It's understood that the current ratio of males to females with autism is 4 to 1, however, new studies suggests it could be as low as 2 to 1. While this gap is slowly closing, women over the age of forty are becoming one of the key groups to seek diagnosis.

item.title: Australia's major employment services shake-up: Here's what's changing — and what's not
item.description: The $312 million overhaul maintains one element critics say is "punishment as usual".

item.title: Australia's major employment services shake-up: Here's what's changing — and what's not
item.description: The $312 million overhaul maintains one element critics say is "punishment as usual".

item.title: 'Not far away': Rescue team confident of reaching group trapped for days in Laos cave
item.description: The team involved in the 2018 Thai cave rescue is part of the Laotian efforts.

item.title: Inflation falls as fuel prices ease — but rate rise fears remain
item.description: Headline inflation fell in April thanks to a cut in the fuel excise, but the underlying impulse is still gathering steam.

item.title: 'I know you are busy': Students apologetic, 'short-changed' as university class sizes explode
item.description: Universities have been accused of compromising Australian education, with a major report showing class sizes have skyrocketed since COVID-19.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 27 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: As the UK faces unprecedented heatwaves, how did the world's biggest cities cope in the past?
item.description: From fire hydrants to rooftops, cities around the world have developed very different methods for staying cool in a heatwave.

item.title: Businesses pass on fuel costs despite easing headline inflation
item.description: Australia’s sharemarket rebounded after a softer-than-expected inflation report lifted hopes the RBA may hold interest rates in June, but ABS data showed some businesses are passing on higher fuel costs to consumers. SBS On the Money explores what the latest CPI figures mean for households, the Reserve Bank and investors, with analysis from Sunny Nguyen, Head of Australia Economics at Moody’s Analytics, and Niv Dagan from Peak Asset Management.

item.title: Eating from bins, watered down food: Desperate ways Aussies are trying to make ends meet
item.description: Food charities say there's an emerging group of Australians seeking food relief for the first time.

item.title: Hamas confirms Israeli strikes killed its new military leader in Gaza
item.description: Thousands of people gathered in Gaza City for the joint funeral of Mohammed Odeh and members of his family.

item.title: Hamas confirms Israeli strikes killed its new military leader in Gaza
item.description: Thousands of people gathered in Gaza City for the joint funeral of Mohammed Odeh and members of his family.

item.title: Newly elected Solomon Islands prime minister set to visit Australia in first official trip
item.description: Matthew Wale was elected prime minister in May following a no-confidence vote against his predecessor.

item.title: IS-linked woman attempted to board flight to Australia despite travel ban
item.description: The woman had been issued a temporary exclusion order and was stopped at the airport in Damascus.

item.title: Weeks in the dark, breathing helium: The Australians doing one of the world's weirdest jobs
item.description: Saturation diving could be the world’s most intriguing, dangerous and isolating job. Reg and Chris explain why they think it’s also the best.

item.title: 'Nobody really knows': Australia's property market has taken on a strange new mood
item.description: Homebuyers are navigating a market unlike anything seen in a very long time, an expert says.

item.title: Reform or rebrand? Advocates say employment system is still unfit for work
item.description: The government has announced what it has described as the biggest reform to employment services in over 30 years. The system has been set up to help unemployed Australians find work. But with the continuation of mutual obligations and private providers, advocates say the changes are nothing more than a rebrand.

item.title: Seven dead, officials on alert as Europe swelters through spring that feels like summer
item.description: Authorities in France say at least seven people have died due to unseasonably warm temperatures, while for the second day in a row, the UK has recorded a new record high for May. Much of Western Europe is still dealing with a pre-summer heatwave, leading to health warnings across the continent.

item.title: Will Australia follow the US and Canada on Ebola travel restrictions?
item.description: The Department of Health says it is "closely monitoring" the situation.

item.title: Federal government sues 3M over PFAS contamination
item.description: The Australian government is suing multinational industrial company 3M in the largest ever claim brought by the Commonwealth. The legal action will seek damages in relation to PFAS contamination across defence sites throughout Australia, alleging the company withheld information about their products.

item.title: Detainees at US immigration centre on hunger strike amid concern over conditions, high suicide rates
item.description: Hundreds of people in an immigration detention centre in the US state of New Jersey are on a hunger strike, according to their families. They're protesting poor conditions inside the Delaney Hall facility. It comes as an investigation found 10 people have died by suicide in immigration detention since President Donald Trump took office at the beginning of last year and launched an aggressive deportation strategy.

item.title: Labor moves to push through controversial tax reforms despite backlash
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers introduced what he called a "bill for workers, first home buyers and future generations" to parliament.

item.title: 3M to 'defend ourselves' in historic $2 billion legal challenge over 'forever chemicals'
item.description: The Australian government says the US manufacturer withheld information about its toxic products that have caused health and land damage.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 28 May 2026
item.description: A daily five-minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Iran-US peace talks stall, Türkiye targets dissent & Trump’s excellent excuse
item.description: Conflicting reports on peace negotiations between the US and Iran as military tensions escalate across the region. Former political prisoner Dr Kylie Moore-Gilbert joins us to explore what it means for the Iranian regime and its citizens. Plus, Netanyahu vows to crush Hezbollah with an expanding offensive as his own leadership comes under pressure ahead of elections. While in Türkiye, Erdogan moves against the opposition in a fresh crackdown on dissent, and Trump’s patriotic excuse for dodging a family function.

item.title: Fire in Kenya girls' school dormitory kills 16, injures 73
item.description: Local authorities said the fire broke out in a dormitory where 220 girls had been sleeping.

item.title: Is 'baby brain' real? Nearly 300 parents put the theory to the test
item.description: Many new parents describe the mental fog of early parenthood as 'baby brain'. But Australian researchers found a surprise.

item.title: 'For us, by us': Indigenous plea for truth-telling and connection during Reconciliation Week
item.description: Community led events are being held across the country to mark National Reconciliation Week, which runs from May 27 to Jun 03. This year's theme is 'All In' - a call for all Australians to commit wholeheartedly to reconciliation, and to advancing Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people's rights.

item.title: Man sentenced for plotting attack on Taylor Swift Vienna concert
item.description: In his testimony last month, the 21-year-old said he had become convinced he "had to wage jihad".

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu says Israel will expand control of Gaza to 70 per cent
item.description: The Israeli prime minister said increasing control of Palestinian territory will "press" Hamas from "all sides".

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 29 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: How a major shock reshaped Australia and the key question we now face
item.description: How did the fuel price surge reshape daily life? More than 2,000 Australians offer some clues.

item.title: ASX soars on US-Iran ceasefire hopes, Australia’s richest people
item.description: Following Thursday’s $45 billion wipeout, the S&P/ASX 200 has finished the week 1.62 per cent higher following reports that US and Iranian negotiators have reached a tentative deal to extend the ceasefire by 60 days. As a result, oil prices fell sharply while gold and silver rose. For more, Stephanie Youssef spoke with Opal Capital Management Chief Investment Officer Omkar Joshi. Also, the combined wealth of Australia's 200 richest people has surged to more than $700 billion, with mining magnate Gina Rinehart once again leading the Australian Financial Review’s 2026 Rich List. For more, Stephanie Youssef spoke with AFR’s Rich List Deputy Editor Hannah Tattersall.

item.title: Australia's resource boom created billions. Did the country miss its chance?
item.description: Australia's resources boom prompts questions about public benefit and whether Norway offers useful lessons.

item.title: Rate rise reprieve, a warning for Australians — and the everyday cost going up
item.description: The key money stories this week, broken down simply and what they mean for you.

item.title: Has climbing Mount Everest become too commercialised and dangerous?
item.description: Nepal's mountaineering community is celebrating the 73rd anniversary of the conquest of Mount Everest amid growing concerns about overcrowding, rising temperatures. Nepal has issued a record 494 permits for foreign climbers, with the number of people who reached the summit believed to be more than 900. Questions are growing over whether the world's highest mountain is becoming too dangerous, and too commercialised.

item.title: Retailers under attack from both weak demand, and rising costs
item.description: Should the conflict continue, higher prices and a shortage of certain goods would have knock-on effects.

item.title: 'A battle of resilience': Why these teachers and youngsters are looking at karate differently
item.description: In a Sydney dojo, karate is being used to build more than physical strength.

item.title: House prices are falling. So why are many Australians still struggling to buy?
item.description: Borrowing power is shrinking faster than house prices, leaving many buyers no closer to owning a home.

item.title: Australian flotilla participants join ICC case, alleging abuse in Israeli custody
item.description: Eleven Australians were detained by Israel while on board a flotilla attempting to deliver aid to Gaza.

item.title: Autism may be as common in women as men. Yet many go undiagnosed
item.description: Autism has long been seen as a condition affecting boys. Researchers say that understanding is finally changing.

item.title: Who might make or miss out on Popovic's Socceroos World Cup squad?
item.description: Several Socceroos remain locked in a battle for places as Popovic nears his World Cup squad call.

item.title: When Mary Rose loses control of her body, it's not a doctor her husband calls
item.description: In the Philippines, priests are increasingly carrying out exorcisms. But mental health experts are worried about misdiagnosis.

item.title: Dating apps are turning to AI. These Australians have a better idea
item.description: Dating apps have been losing users in recent years. What's emerging, experts say, isn't just a trend.

item.title: The ancient Japanese skills helping young people combat modern stress
item.description: Almost half of all teenagers in Australia are facing psychological distress, according to recent studies. Japanese martial arts skills are helping some build resilience.

item.title: Report calls for more federal support for new dads
item.description: A report calling for more federal support for new dads was tabled in federal parliament this week by the Special Envoy for Men's Health Dan Repacholi. The report by the Movember Institute surveyed 1,200 Australian fathers, finding 84 per cent said fatherhood gave their life greater meaning and 77 per cent said it helped them express love and vulnerability more. But three of five new fathers said they were never asked about their mental health, while one in five reported feeling more isolated or lonely since becoming a father.

item.title: SBS Speaks to Dr Marianne Jauncey about NSW's only medically supervised injecting centre
item.description: New South Wales’ only medically supervised injecting centre is calling for urgent legal reform to allow more sites to open across the state. It has been 25 years since the centre opened in Sydney’s Kings Cross, becoming the first supervised injecting facility in the Southern Hemisphere. Staff have since supervised more than 1.3 million injections, managed more than 12-thousand overdoses without a single death, and made more than 25-thousand referrals for treatment and care. For more, SBS spoke with the centre's Medical Director Dr Marianne Jauncey.

item.title: Trump says he will headline US birthday event himself after artists drop out
item.description: Several musical performers scheduled to appear in the celebration have cancelled, citing concerns about its association with the US president.

item.title: 'They represent an era': Why football tracksuits are more than just kits
item.description: Here's how football continues to help some migrants hold onto pieces of home abroad.

item.title: Drones and second-hand submarines: US 'grateful' for Australia as latest AUKUS details unveiled
item.description: The US has again demanded its allies step up their military budgets at a high-level international defence meeting in Singapore.

item.title: Devastating storm set to move across Australia after leaving thousands in WA without power
item.description: The powerful weather event is being described as a "once in five-year" storm and is bringing winds of up to 125kph.

item.title: When Lisa was diagnosed with dementia in her 40s, her partner Luke had to 'beg' for support
item.description: Younger-onset dementia is rare but can occur when some are still looking after children and accessing government support can be difficult.

item.title: 'Very excited': New discovery in Laos cave rescue as Aussie diver outlines next steps
item.description: South Australian diver Josh Richards says miners freed from a flooded cave in Laos had provided valuable information to rescuers.

item.title: Ceasefire hopes fade as tensions persist
item.description: Despite weeks of diplomacy and repeated claims a broader agreement is close, conflict continues across the Middle East. Israeli forces have expanded operations in Lebanon, civilians have again been killed in Gaza, and uncertainty remains over negotiations between Iran and the United States.

item.title: Substandard? Why Australia will now only get second-hand submarines under AUKUS deal
item.description: The Deputy Prime Minister has announced a significant shift in the AUKUS deal, with Australia to purchase three second hand nuclear submarines and move to focus on new unmanned underwater drones. The shift comes amid doubts over US capability to produce enough new vessels, prompting concerns that Australia will receive less capable and shorter lasting submarines.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 01 June 2026
What to know about Beaufort Castle — the 900-year-old site seized by Israel in Lebanon
item.description: The fortress has been a strategic military asset for centuries.

item.title: House prices fall in Sydney and Melbourne
item.description: The cost of buying a home in Australia's two biggest cities has dropped, according to the latest figures from research agency Cotality. Dwelling values fell 0.9 per cent in Sydney and 0.8 per cent in Melbourne during May. There was also a drop in the ACT, of 0.2 per cent. While prices rose in other state and territory capitals, the growth was weaker than previously seen.

item.title: Labor's problem, One Nation's gain: What's driving the poll surge
item.description: One Nation is now the nation's most popular party a new poll suggests, which could offer lessons for the government.

item.title: Popovic's big calls as he reveals Socceroos World Cup squad
item.description: Tony Popovic has named his 26-player Socceroos squad for the FIFA World Cup 2026™ — and there are some big surprises. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, which breaks down the squad, the notable omissions and the selections that could define Australia's World Cup campaign. Why did Cristian Volpato make the cut after previously turning down the Socceroos? What does the inclusion of striker Tete Yengi say about Popovic's plans? And can this young Australian side make it beyond the FIFA World Cup 2026™ group stage?

item.title: Home prices in reverse while Australians rethink retirement
item.description: The Australian sharemarket finished flat ahead of key economic data this week, including GDP figures and US jobs numbers. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Elio D’Amato from EnviroInvest about the day’s market moves, rising oil prices and BHP’s record high. Plus, Kelly Power, CEO of CFS Superannuation, explains why Australians now believe they need more than $1 million to retire comfortably and why many expect to work longer before leaving the workforce. Also, housing expert Tim Lawless warns national property prices could begin falling from next month as higher interest rates, a slowing economy and policy changes start to weigh on the market.

item.title: Israel orders troops further into Lebanon following day of heavy fire by Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli troops have pushed further into Lebanon despite the ceasefire announced six weeks ago.

item.title: Melbourne's love of head-to-toe black clothing actually makes sense. Here's why
item.description: Melbourne has built a reputation for its love of black outfits. It's not a random phenomenon.

item.title: The Australian fans paying up to $25,000 for their FIFA World Cup 2026 'dream'
item.description: Soaring flight, accommodation and ticket prices have not deterred thousands of Australians heading to this year's FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Climate change 'loading the dice' as farmers brace for El Niño announcement
item.description: A formal El Niño declaration could pivot many Australian regions back into prolonged dry spells.

item.title: Australia's population has hit 28 million. These are the places growing fastest
item.description: It's thought Australia could reach 30 million people in the next few years.

item.title: Wage umpire's 'regret' over minimum pay rise as unions cheer, employers cry foul
item.description: Unions had been calling for a 6 per cent increase, which employers branded a "ludicrous" ask.

item.title: Australia sanctions Israeli extremists in response to violence in Palestine
item.description: The new sanctions come over a week after Australia sanctioned Israel's national security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 02 June 2026
Global concerns grow over Israel's military campaign in Lebanon
item.description: Lebanon says Hezbollah has accepted a US-backed proposal for a reciprocal halt to attacks with Israel, as the UN and world powers voice alarm over Israeli strikes in densely populated Beirut suburbs. The United Nations Security Council has held an emergency meeting over the escalation of Israel's military campaign in Lebanon, a day before Lebanon and Israel are to begin a fourth round of US-hosted direct negotiations.

item.title: Pay rise for Australia's lowest earners
item.description: Millions of Australian employees are set to receive a 4.75 per cent wage increase in July, after the Fair Work Commission handed down its annual wage review. Union groups have welcomed the figure, saying it will allow workers to get back on their feet. Business groups, on the other hand, say the move will worsen inflation and put too much pressure on businesses.

item.title: Six hour delays: Fresh warning for Aussies travelling to Europe
item.description: The rollout of the EU's new digital identity system has caused some travellers to miss flights.

item.title: Minimum wage workers get a pay rise as Anthropic looks to list on the sharemarket
item.description: Around three million low-paid Australian workers are set to receive a pay rise after the Fair Work Commission lifted the national minimum wage and increased award wages above the current inflation rate. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Commonwealth Bank economist Harry Ottley about what the decision means for household budgets, inflation and the broader economy. Plus, the ASX 200 edged lower as retailers and shopping centre operators came under pressure following the wage decision, while technology stocks rallied and BHP hit another record high. Alice Shen, CFA from VanEck joins the podcast to unpack the day’s market moves and the influence of another strong session on Wall Street

item.title: Major Russian bombardment of Ukraine kills 11, after Ukraine denies dormitory strike
item.description: Russia warned ‌that the "systemic strikes" are in response to a drone attack on a Luhansk dormitory, which Ukraine denies.

item.title: CSIRO confirms significant job cuts due to rising science costs
item.description: Executive staff said they can meet climate change research obligations despite restricting their workforce.

item.title: The blue-tongued lizard at the heart of one school's reconciliation journey
item.description: Behind one school's blue-tongued lizard lies a deeper story about kinship and Country.

item.title: Drone attack kills one, forces closure of Kuwait airport as Iran and US trade strikes
item.description: Kuwait's foreign ministry said the strikes caused damage to vital infrastructure including diplomatic missions.

item.title: Drone attack kills one, forces closure of Kuwait airport as Iran and US trade strikes
item.description: Kuwait's foreign ministry said the strikes caused damage to vital infrastructure including diplomatic missions.

item.title: Drone attack kills one, forces closure of Kuwait airport as Iran and US trade strikes
item.description: Kuwait's foreign ministry said the strikes caused damage to vital infrastructure including diplomatic missions.

item.title: Caucus raucous over AUKUS as Labor backbencher casts doubt on submarines
item.description: Australia was to receive a mix of new and used submarines. Now, it will only get used ones.

item.title: 'Some kind of apocalypse': 40,000 people shelter in Kyiv metro amid major Russian attack
item.description: Russian attacks killed at least 22 people across Ukraine overnight. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has warned more attacks could follow.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 03 June 2026
'Paid for a Porsche, got a compact two-seater': Is this AUKUS' hidden cost?
item.description: Experts say used submarines don't change Australia's AUKUS goals, but critics think we're getting ripped off.

item.title: 'Australia has failed': US opens door to new tariffs in trade crackdown
item.description: Donald Trump's administration has been scrambling to rebuild its tariff agenda after a US supreme court decision.

item.title: Australia's economy stalls despite data centre investment boom
item.description: Australia’s economy expanded by just 0.3% in the March quarter, with growth driven largely by investment in data centres while household spending remained subdued and productivity declined. NAB Senior Markets Economist Taylor Nugent joins SBS On the Money to unpack what the latest GDP figures mean for the economy and the outlook for interest rates. Plus, despite the weak economic data, the ASX 200 climbed 0.7% as investors scaled back expectations of a near-term rate rise. Josh Gilbert from eToro explains what drove the market higher, including record highs for mining giants BHP and Rio Tinto, strong gains for the banks and a pullback in technology stocks.

item.title: The World Cup is finding out what fans are worth in football's new economy
item.description: Some fans have been priced out of the World Cup in North America, which some critics say is part of a wider shift in sporting culture.

item.title: More of these cars are entering Australia — and they've sparked a burning question
item.description: Experts are warning that popular vehicles pose a major security concern.

item.title: Iran war is over, Rubio says, as strikes continue
item.description: Opponents argue that the war is still raging, with strikes on Kuwait overnight.

item.title: Medicinal cannabis patients given green light to drive in NSW
item.description: The state government is opening up legal access to the roads for patients who have taken cannabis medication.

item.title: The Australian scientists uncovering the origins of the Milky Way
item.description: CSIRO scientists have used a powerful radio telescope in Western Australia to map magnetic fields for clues to the galaxy's origins.

item.title: 'Hidden costs': AI data centres set to consume more water than every person on Earth by 2030
item.description: A United Nations report has highlighted the vast and growing demand that artificial intelligence is placing on the world.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement conditional ceasefire
item.description: It comes days after Israel signalled a major escalation in its ongoing invasion of southern Lebanon.

item.title: Albanese, Taylor hit back at Trump tariff threat as US blasts 'unacceptable' failures
item.description: There is an "ideological disagreement" between Australia and the US on tariffs, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has said.

item.title: Elderly couple critical after home invasion in northern NSW | Midday News Bulletin 04 June 2026
item.description: An elderly couple fight for life after a violent home invasion in northern New South Wales; Israel and Lebanon strike a new US-backed ceasefire deal; And in sport; Italian Matteo Arnaldi in his first Grand Slam semi-final at the French Open.

item.title: 'Not the act of a friendly country': Bipartisan anger erupts over 'rotten' US tariff plan
item.description: The United States has proposed a new set of tariffs on 60 trading partners, including Australia, alleging they have failed to impose and enforce forced labour bans in their supply chains. The Australian government has rejected the accusations and says the proposed 12.5 per cent import tax contravenes the free trade agreement.

item.title: Racial furore erupts after video emerges of UK police handcuffing dying stab victim
item.description: A WARNING THAT THIS STORY MAY BE DISTRESSING FOR SOME LISTENERS Britain's interior minister has accused activists of hijacking a tragedy to stir up violence after the death of a teenager who was handcuffed as he lay dying - while his killer falsely claimed a racist attack. Nowak's death has triggered debates about policing and knife crime and has spurred claims by far-right activists and politicians that there is bias against white people in the justice system.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 04 June 2026
'Pacific ashtray': Fiji rejects Australian billionaire's plan to ship and burn waste
item.description: A proposal to ship non-recyclable rubbish to Fiji from across the region for burning has been labelled "waste colonialism".

item.title: Australia is fuming over Trump's tariffs, but the trade fight has a darker backdrop
item.description: The US president's threat may be opportunistic but the concerns aren't entirely baseless, experts say.

item.title: Tax changes pass the House of Representatives | Evening News Bulletin 04 June 2026
item.description: The government's tax changes pass the House of Representatives, but face a challenge in the Senate; Australian Sumud Flotilla members call for further action against Israel; And in sport, Socceroos defender Lucas Herrington boosted by his coach's public vote of confidence ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: 'Horrific': Penny Wong grilled on investigation into alleged sexual assault of flotilla activist
item.description: Fronting a Senate estimates hearing, the foreign minister was also asked how many people Australia has helped to leave Gaza.

item.title: Can you negatively gear a granny flat? Labor has given an answer
item.description: Questions raised during Senate estimates about negative gearing and granny flats have finally been answered.

item.title: Trump’s Bibi tirade, Hegseth peddles used subs &… lucky testicles?
item.description: Donald Trump loses his patience with Benjamin Netanyahu over Lebanon, while the Israeli prime minister hints at an even bigger endgame in Gaza. Is a new Middle East deal within reach, or the region’s latest escalation heading to another full conflict? Plus, a surprise AUKUS announcement on the sidelines of the Shangri-La dialogue and how an iconic Italian artwork is hanging in there.

item.title: Queensland children under five to be removed from residential state care
item.description: A Queensland Commission of Inquiry has delivered a damning report into the state's child protection system. It detailed evidence of sexual abuse in residential care settings, and delivered 52 recommendations.The government has announced it will remove all children under five from residential care, and respond to the recommendations within two months.

item.title: SpaceX’s mega IPO lands as miners drag the market lower
item.description: Elon Musk has revealed plans for a potentially record-breaking IPO of SpaceX, with Australian retail investors set to get access to the offer. Stephanie Youssef speaks with Datt Capital Chief Investment Officer Emanuel Datt about the opportunity and the risks for investors. Plus, the Australian sharemarket falls 1.1 per cent as mining stocks lead the losses, with Robert Talevski from Activam Group breaking down the day’s market moves and what drove the sell-off.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill nine in Gaza, despite ceasefire
item.description: At least 15 others were wounded in overnight attacks that targeted four apartments.

item.title: The US is celebrating 250 years. But whose version of America wins?
item.description: Trump is turning what is meant to be a national celebration into a spectacle about himself and his movement, experts say.

item.title: Why this year’s FIFA World Cup ball is unlike any used before
item.description: FIFA is expanding its use of AI and tracking technology to help officials with decisions.

item.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon truce | Morning News Bulletin 5 June 2026
item.description: Hezbollah rejects ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon; Australia's foreign minister backs four New Zealand politicians banned by China over a trip to Taiwan; Poland's Maja Chwalinska books her first Grand Slam final spot in the French Open.

item.title: Zelenskyy seizes opportunity as Russia concedes weaknesses in air defence
item.description: Zelenskyy's proposal comes after heavy Ukranian drone attacks on St. Petersburg prompted the Kremlin's surprising admission.

item.title: Trump announces $981 million support package for highly polluting energy source
item.description: The White House has hosted politicians and governors from coal-rich states to announce further support for "beautiful, clean coal."

item.title: The old habit Australians are refusing to abandon
item.description: A new national survey reveals which cultural habits Australians are sticking with.

item.title: US accuses UK of having 'two-tier policing' system amid unrest over student's murder
item.description: Keir Starmer has hit back at criticism from Elon Musk as the White House joins criticism around the UK's policing policy.

item.title: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire in Lebanon as Trump maintains the deal holds
item.description: Hezbollah has rejected a US-backed renewed ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon. The leader of the Iranian-backed group says the terms were negotiated in talks that they were excluded from - and they amount to surrender.

item.title: Hanson steps in after Joyce backflips on forced home sales
item.description: After failing to answer questions, a senator was warned to "get some clarity before this gets worse".

item.title: Government pushes back on Coalition housing concerns | Midday News Bulletin 05 June 2026
item.description: Donald Trump says he'd he honoured to meet with Iran's Supreme Leader amid pressure to end the war; Labor hits back at the opposition on permanent resident housing help; The Socceroos prepare for final World Cup trial match.

item.title: Government pushes back on Coalition housing concerns | Midday News Bulletin 05 June 2026
item.description: Donald Trump says he'd he honoured to meet with Iran's Supreme Leader amid pressure to end the war; Labor hits back at the opposition on permanent resident housing help; The Socceroos prepare for final World Cup trial match.

item.title: Hezbollah rejects Israeli ceasefire deal in Lebanon, clouding prospects for Iran conflict resolution
item.description: Iran has declared that any ceasefire deal in the Middle East conflict between itself and the United States must include Lebanon.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 05 June 2026
Australian content creator weighs legal options after sacking over contentious video
item.description: Lisa Jane Spencer has rejected accusations of racism over a video featuring a fictional First Nations character.

item.title: Albanese denies he tried to hide tax changes from Australians | Evening News Bulletin 05 June 2026
item.description: Anthony Albanese denies he tried to hide tax changes from Australians at the last election; The Opposition demands more human involvement in the allocation of aged care packages; And in sport, the Socceroos say they're working on the little things ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: Bondi hero Ahmed al Ahmed's denial after being charged with assault
item.description: Al Ahmed became a national hero after he disarmed one of two gunmen during the 2025 Bondi Beach terrorist incident.

item.title: Budget hangs in the balance amid questions over tax and the NDIS
item.description: The Federal Government, Greens and Coalition are gridlocked over post-budget measures on tax and the NDIS - with just one sitting fortnight remaining before Parliament rises for a five-week Winter break. As negotiations continue, participants in the NDIS are facing an anxious wait, with the scheme set for a major overhaul, including significant cuts.

item.title: Tumbling miners lead ASX to a down week
item.description: The ASX 200 closed 0.7% lower on Friday, extending its weekly decline to 1.2%, as mining stocks came under pressure from a three-month low in iron ore prices. Fortescue, BHP and Rio Tinto all fell, while the major banks also weakened. Bucking the trend was healthcare giant CSL, which jumped 5.8% for its strongest gain in more than four years. SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Blueberry Markets analyst Zoran Kresovic about the forces driving the market and what investors are watching next.

item.title: As home sellers slash prices, one of Sydney's richest suburbs records the biggest discount
item.description: Despite price falls in certain suburbs, an economist says one factor of the property market remains unchanged.

item.title: Sell before 2027 or hold forever? Australians weigh impact of CGT changes
item.description: Tax changes unveiled in the budget have rattled many Australians. Here's what to consider if you want to sell an asset.

item.title: The $1 million question facing Australians planning for retirement
item.description: New retirement findings highlight growing financial concerns. Plus: Incoming pay rises, and Australia's property market shift.

item.title: Iran links US peace deal to Lebanon conflict | | Morning News Bulletin 6 June 2026
item.description: Iran warns it will widen the conflict with the US if attacks resume; the Prime Minister says the rise of One Nation wasa factor in his tax reform proposal; and Alexander Zverev books a French Open final showdown with Flavio Cobolli

item.title: This rapper reached half a billion streams with one song. He's still forging his identity
item.description: Sooraj Cherukat, known as Hanumankind, says he's caught between 'multiple worlds'. But he doesn't want other people defining him.

item.title: Torres Strait’s first land and sea conference weaves culture with science
item.description: Traditional owners in Far North Queensland have come together with scientists for the first time to help tackle threats against human-induced climate change. The inaugural conference with leaders from seventeen different Torres Strait Islands is helping shape Australia's next State of Environment Report due in December this year. Reporter Josh van Staden travelled to Thursday Island with support from the Pulitzer Center.

item.title: Iran slams US 'discriminatory treatment' over World Cup visa refusals
item.description: The players had their visas granted just ten days out from the tournament.

item.title: Interview: Dr Sam Bennett from Grattan Institute on future of the NDIS
item.description: Australians who use the National Disability Insurance Scheme are facing an anxious wait with political gridlock in Canberra. The Federal Government, Greens and Coalition have been considering post-budget measures on tax and the NDIS and there's just one sitting fortnight remaining before Parliament rises for a five-week Winter break. As negotiations continue, Tee Mitchell spoke with Dr Sam Bennett, the Disability Program Director at the Grattan Institute. Dr Bennett starts by addressing the elephant in the room which is what might happen if the proposed changes don't make it through the parliament.

item.title: Putin says 'no point' meeting with Zelenskyy, vowing to double down on war efforts
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had called for a sit-down between the two leaders to end the four-year war.

item.title: Which states get a public holiday for the King's Birthday on Monday?
item.description: Most states and territories will enjoy a long weekend over the coming days, but two jurisdictions differ.

item.title: US shoots down four Iranian drones launched towards the Strait of Hormuz | Midday News Bulletin 6 June 2026
item.description: Tensions escalate between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz; Australia-New Zealand security talks begin; and Jessica Fox storms back from surgery to win World Cup kayak gold.

item.title: US shoots down four Iranian drones launched towards the Strait of Hormuz | Midday News Bulletin 6 June 2026
item.description: Tensions escalate between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz; Australia-New Zealand security talks begin; and Jessica Fox storms back from surgery to win World Cup kayak gold.

item.title: US shoots down four Iranian drones launched towards the Strait of Hormuz | Midday News Bulletin 6 June 2026
item.description: Tensions escalate between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz; Australia-New Zealand security talks begin; and Jessica Fox storms back from surgery to win World Cup kayak gold.

item.title: Whose choice is it? The unseen side of Australia's abortion debate
item.description: Some pregnant people face harassment and bullying from family members pushing for termination.

item.title: US says Iranian coastal radar sites struck after drones threaten shipping
item.description: The latest exchange of strikes in the Gulf threatens efforts to end the three-month war.

item.title: Leaders of Australia and NZ discuss deeper co-operation with Pacific nations | Evening Bulletin 6 June 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, The leaders of Australia and New Zealand discuss deeper co-operation with Pacific nations; Thousands gather for the Barunga Festival in the Northern Territory to celebrate First Nations culture; and in cycling, French rider Celia Gery wins stage seven of the women's Giro d'Italia.

item.title: Australia's fuel supply secured well into August as bowser pressure eases
item.description: But with the government's fuel tax set to increase again, the relief could be temporary.

item.title: How the murder of a British teen became an international flashpoint
item.description: Despite an appeal from Henry Nowak's family, right-wing politicians including Pauline Hanson and JD Vance have seized on the case.

item.title: Public health agency concerned latest Ebola outbreak could become largest on record
item.description: The World Health Organisation says the outbreak is 'moving fast'.

item.title: Ukraine launches drone attack on Russia's second biggest city | Morning Bulletin 7 June 2026
item.description: Ukraine launches a second major drone attack on Russia's second-largest city; a man dies after being bitten by a shark off the W-A coast; and in tennis, teenager Mirra Andreeva claims her first Grand Slam title at the French Open.

item.title: This island's population is the same as Darwin's. It's about to play in the World Cup
item.description: Curaçao has the same population as Darwin — and it's competing in the World Cup. What could the 'Blue Wave' mean for its future?

item.title: People keep disappearing in this region of China. Yalkun's father is one of them
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals the changing nature of so-called 're-education camps' in the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region.

item.title: Using his culture to 'heal him' - how Samuel Nai is recovering after a near-fatal shark attack
item.description: A teenager, who was attacked by a shark in Far North Queensland last year, has made a stunning recovery after months in hospital and multiple surgeries. SBS travelled to Thursday Island in the Torres Strait, where Samuel Nai is back on country, practising his culture to help him heal.

item.title: Players sent off, matches abandoned: FIFA to crack down on discrimination at the World Cup
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 could be a test for the organisation and its commitment to rid football of racism. In 2024, FIFA launched its 'Global Stand Against Racism' with a series of initiatives, including an anti-racism panel, education campaigns, and introducing tougher rules to combat discrimination. With incidents of racism continuing to blight professional football while FIFA prepares for its biggest tournament ever in Mexico, Canada and the US, here's how the footballing organisation's policies may play out.

item.title: Socceroos hold Switzerland to a draw in World Cup warm-up after Yengi scores on debut
item.description: Australia were under sustained pressure for much of the match, but came out stronger after the half-time break.

item.title: Hegseth warns of migrant 'invasion' of Europe in D-Day memorial speech
item.description: His comments come days after US vice president JD Vance blamed immigration for the murder of UK teenager Henry Nowak.

item.title: Manhunt underway after 12 injured in shooting at street festival in US state of Ohio
item.description: The shooting took place in Toledo on Saturday afternoon local time, with police saying several people had been injured.

item.title: US-Iran war approaches 100 day milestone | Midday Bulletin 7 June 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, US-Iran war approaches it's 100 day milestone, pressure mounts to appoint a Queensland children’s commissioner; and in sport, the Socceroos draw with Switzerland in final their World Cup warm-up.

item.title: Israeli troops shoot dead seven-month-old Palestinian baby in the occupied West Bank
item.description: Israeli military and settler violence against Palestinians in the occupied territories has surged since the Gaza war began.

item.title: 'The crazy Chilean' was almost defeated by her own football team. Now she fights for change
item.description: Former Chilean goalkeeper Romina Parraguirre now helps FIFA confront racism ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: PM defends AUKUS deal amid criticism | Evening News Bulletin 7 June 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, The prime minister defends the AUKUS deal against criticism it could increase tensions with China; Pope Leo becomes the first pontiff to visit Spain in 15 years; and in cycling, Dutch rider Demi Vollering wins stage 8 of the women's Giro d'Italia.

item.title: 'See what happens': PM takes aim at Coalition over 'divisive' permanent resident debate
item.description: Both One Nation and the Coalition have proposed policies affecting permanent residents, including on housing and social services.

item.title: 'You can change history': Pope Leo appeals to youth in first papal visit to Spain in 15 years
item.description: Pope Leo has kicked off a six-day tour of Spain, where he has met with high-level officials ahead of an expected address to parliament. The pontiff has addressed the rise of artificial intelligence, political populism, and global conflict during his visit. It's the first time a pope has visited Spain in 15 years.

item.title: King's Birthday Honours 2026: See the full list of awardees
item.description: From sport and the arts to academia and industry, the King's Birthday Honours celebrate Australia's best. See the full list.

item.title: Hundreds of community heroes celebrated in the 2026 King's Birthday Honours
item.description: More than 900 Australians have been recognised for their exceptional contribution to the country in the 2026 King's Birthday Honours List. Here, SBS spotlights some of the many community heroes celebrated with a Medal of the Order of Australia. Among them are a self-defence teacher, a powerful advocate for victims of crime, and an author celebrating historic achievements of Greek women.

item.title: Lindy Chamberlain says jurors 'didn't understand' the evidence in her case. She wants change
item.description: Twelve members of the community can decide the fate of those on trial. Is the jury system still effective?

item.title: Iranian missiles target northern Israel | Morning Bulletin 8 June 2026
item.description: Iranian missiles target northern Israel; One Nation tops the latest Newspoll as Labor and the Coalition slide; Alexander Zverev finally breaks through for his first career major title at the French Open.

item.title: Trump urges Netanyahu to hold back after Iranian retaliation as war enters 100th day
item.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon's capital have enraged Iran, as the US-Israeli-triggered war in the Middle East reaches its 100th day.

item.title: Trump urges Netanyahu to hold back after Iranian retaliation as war enters 100th day
item.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon's capital have enraged Iran, as the US-Israeli-triggered war in the Middle East reaches its 100th day.

item.title: 'Disgusted': Albanese, Gillard condemn 'Ditch the Witch' campaign targeting Allan
item.description: A billboard truck spotted in Melbourne was displaying the same slogan infamously used against former prime minister Julia Gillard.

item.title: Albanese offers state funeral for Scolyer as family mourns the 'big hole' in their lives
item.description: The 2024 Australian of the Year said he would like to be remembered as "a proud everyday Aussie who gave it a crack".

item.title: Tsunami warnings issued as 7.8 magnitude earthquake strikes southern Philippines
item.description: Tsunami warnings project one metre waves will begin to arrive in various regions from 11.30am local time.
